name: Ulwa

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Ulwa (ulwa1239).

notes: Misumalpan (Southern Sumu) language of Karawala, Nicaragua, in the practical orthography of the Ulwa dictionary. Three vowel qualities a/i/u each contrast for length (written doubled); ng is the velar nasal. Stress is predictable (first syllable if heavy, otherwise second) and so is not written. Phonemic voiceless sonorants (voiceless m n ŋ l r and voiceless glides) are not given distinct letters in the practical spelling, so voicelessness is not recovered, which is the main PER gap for this skeleton entry.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
